key_topics: Number: counting and ordering numbers to 20
Reading and writing numbers to 20 in numerals and words
Comparing numbers: more, fewer, less, greater than, less than
Number bonds: pairs of numbers that make up to 10
Addition and subtraction within 20
Counting on and back in ones, twos, fives, and tens
Odd and even numbers to 20
Simple fractions: half and quarter of shapes and sets
Geometry: 2D shapes (circle, triangle, square, rectangle, pentagon, hexagon)
3D shapes: cube, cuboid, sphere, cone, cylinder
Patterns: repeating patterns with shapes and numbers
Measurement: comparing and ordering lengths, masses, and capacities
Time: days of week, months of year, reading time to the hour
competences: Count, read, and write numbers to 20
Add and subtract one-digit numbers within 20
Identify and sort 2D and 3D shapes by properties
Find halves and quarters of shapes and quantities
Compare and order lengths, masses, and capacities
typical_mistakes: Confusing tens and ones when reading two-digit numbers
Incorrect number bond combinations that don't sum to target
Counting objects more than once or skipping objects
Confusing half (equal parts) with just splitting into two parts

key_topics: Phonics: letter-sound correspondences, consonant blends
Reading simple sentences and short stories with picture support
High-frequency words: recognising and spelling common words
Reading aloud with growing confidence and expression
Comprehension: answering questions about texts
Writing: forming letters correctly, writing words and simple sentences
Capital letters and full stops
Speaking and listening: taking turns, expressing ideas clearly
Vocabulary: building word knowledge through reading and discussion
Grammar: nouns, verbs, adjectives (naming words, doing words, describing words)
competences: Decode and read simple texts using phonics knowledge
Write simple sentences with correct letter formation and punctuation
Answer questions about stories and informational texts
Participate in discussions and take turns speaking
typical_mistakes: Omitting spaces between words when writing
Reading without attention to punctuation (no pausing at full stops)
Guessing words from initial letter only without applying phonics

key_topics: Living things: plants and animals in the local environment
Parts of a plant: root, stem, leaf, flower
Parts of an animal: head, body, legs, wings
Materials: wood, plastic, metal, fabric — sorting by properties
Properties of materials: hard/soft, rough/smooth, shiny/dull
Senses: sight, hearing, touch, taste, smell
Light and dark: sources of light, shadows
Pushes and pulls: how forces move objects
competences: Identify and name parts of plants and animals
Describe objects using the five senses
Investigate pushes and pulls in everyday life
typical_mistakes: Confusing living and non-living things (fire, water as living)
Not distinguishing between a property and a material
